events.event_description: U.S. Legation established in Abyssinia (Ethiopia) when the first Minister Resident/Consul General, Hoffman Philip, presented credentials.

evidence.quote: Hoffman Philip, Minister Resident/Consul General. Appointed: 1908-07-20. Credentials presented: 1909-07-06. Left post on 1910-02-08. Note: Commissioned during a recess of the Senate; recommissioned on December 9, 1908, after confirmation. Commissioned to Abyssinia. Did not serve under the July 20, 1908, appointment.

events.event_description: U.S. Legation effectively closed following Italian occupation of Addis Ababa; Engert left post on May 4, 1937, with no successor until 1943.

evidence.quote: Cornelius Van Hemert Engert, Minister Resident/Consul General. Appointed: 1936-02-07. Credentials presented: 1936-04-30. Italian forces occupied Addis Ababa on 1936-05-06. Note: Engert left post on May 4, 1937.

events.event_description: U.S. Legation re-established in Ethiopia when John Kenneth Caldwell (Minister Resident/Consul General) presented credentials.

evidence.quote: John Kenneth Caldwell, Minister Resident/Consul General. Appointed: 1943-04-14. Credentials presented: 1943-08-31. Promoted to Envoy Extraordinary and Minister Plenipotentiary 1943-12-09.

events.event_description: U.S. Legation elevated to Embassy when George Robert Merrell was promoted to Ambassador Extraordinary and Plenipotentiary and presented credentials.

evidence.quote:
George Robert Merrell, Envoy Extraordinary and Minister Plenipotentiary. Appointed: 1947-05-15. Credentials presented: 1948-01-01. Promoted to Ambassador Extraordinary and Plenipotentiary 1949-06-28.

George Robert Merrell, Ambassador Extraordinary and Plenipotentiary. Appointed: 1949-05-21. Credentials presented: 1949-06-28. Left post on 1951-03-17.
